name: Pecan Penuche
recipeIngredient: Butter or margarine (2 1/2 sticks, small pieces)
recipeInstructions:
Penuche is firmer than classic fudge, but because of its deep, sweet flavor, it's often called brown-sugar fudge.
1. Grease an 8-inch square pan and set aside.
2. In a 3-quart heavy saucepan, stir together the sugar, milk, and salt until well combined.
3. Over medium heat, bring the mixture to a boil, stirring until the sugar has dissolved completely.
4. Cover the pan and continue boiling for 2 to 3 minutes.
5. Uncover the pan and use a pastry brush dipped in cold water to wash down the sides of the pan.
6. Continue boiling over medium heat, stirring only if the mixture starts to burn, until it reaches 244°F (firm-ball stage) on a candy thermometer.
7. Remove from heat and immediately place the saucepan in cold water. Without stirring, add the butter.
8. Let the mixture cool slightly, then beat with a wooden spoon until it begins to thicken.
9. Add the pecans and vanilla, beating until the candy loses some of its gloss.
10. Spread the mixture evenly into the prepared pan and score into 25 squares.
11. Allow to set until firm, then cut through the scored lines.
12. Store in an airtight container. Makes approximately 1-1/2 pounds.
Per square: 105 calories, 1 gram protein, 18 grams carbohydrates, 4 grams fat, 5 mg cholesterol with butter, 1 mg cholesterol with margarine From Woman's Day (1/15/80) Famous Fanny Farmer Candies and Cookies
